ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general doughnut food product class; no specific filled cheesecake donut subclass exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP covers doughnuts as a category; no specific cheesecake-filled variant exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept exists for cheesecake filled donut. None assigned.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard cheesecake filled donut as sold at Dunkin' Donuts, approximately 75 g.

nutrient_source.title: Cake doughnuts, plain (USDA FoodData Central, SR Legacy, NDB No. 18247); cream cheese filling estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for cream cheese (NDB No. 01017); composite estimate for cheesecake-filled donut
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA entry exists for a cheesecake-filled donut. Values are estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy data for a yeast-raised glazed donut (NDB No. 18249) as the base dough component (~60% by weight) with USDA cream cheese data (NDB No. 01017) as the filling component (~40% by weight). The composite per-100g values are derived from this weighted blend. This is a reasonable approximation for a commercially produced cheesecake-filled donut of the Dunkin' Donuts type.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; doughnuts entry (GI=76, glucose reference)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a cheesecake-filled donut. The GI of 76 is taken from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for doughnuts (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care 31:2281-2283), which is the closest analogous food from an approved GI source. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (76 × 32.6) / 100 ≈ 24.8, where carbohydrate per 75g serving = 43.5 × 0.75 = 32.6g.

hypertension.guidance: A single 75g cheesecake filled donut provides approximately 233mg sodium (~10% of the 2,300mg daily limit) and 5.6g saturated fat; individuals with hypertension should limit consumption and account for cumulative sodium from other meals.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (GI 76) and high refined carbohydrate and sugar load
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of 76 and a glycemic load of approximately 24.8 per serving, this donut causes a rapid postprandial blood glucose rise and should be avoided or strictly limited by individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; if consumed, portion should be minimised and paired with protein or fibre to blunt the glycemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat, trans fat, and high sugar content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This product contains approximately 7.4g saturated fat and 0.2g trans fat per 100g alongside 18g sugar, all of which are associated with adverse lipid profiles and increased cardiovascular risk; individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat this as an occasional indulgence and limit serving frequency.

llm_training.natural_language_description: A cheesecake filled donut is a deep-fried yeast-raised or cake-style donut that has been injected or filled with a sweetened cream cheese mixture designed to replicate the flavour and texture of cheesecake. The exterior is typically glazed, sugar-dusted, or left plain, while the interior contains a dense, tangy, and creamy filling made from cream cheese, sugar, and sometimes vanilla. The product sold at Dunkin' Donuts in Singapore under the name 'Cheesecake Filled' is a commercially produced ultra-processed pastry. Nutritionally, it is energy-dense at approximately 348 kcal per 100g, with a significant proportion of calories derived from refined carbohydrates (43.5g/100g), fat (17.2g/100g including 7.4g saturated fat), and added sugars (18g/100g). The glycemic index is high (GI 76), making it a food that causes rapid blood glucose elevation. It is not suitable for regular consumption by individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, cardiovascular disease, or hypertension. The product contains gluten, dairy, and eggs, making it unsuitable for those with relevant allergies or intolerances. In the Singapore food-service context, it is sold as a snack or breakfast item, typically priced around SGD 2.90 per unit at Dunkin' Donuts outlets.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a Dunkin Donuts cheesecake filled donut?
Is cheesecake donut high in sugar?
Can diabetics eat cheesecake filled donuts?
What is the GI of a cheesecake donut?
Cheesecake filled donut nutrition facts Singapore
How much fat is in a cheesecake filled donut?

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for a cheesecake-filled donut exists in any approved primary source. Values are estimated from a weighted composite of USDA SR Legacy data for yeast-raised glazed donut and cream cheese. GI is taken from the closest analogous food (doughnuts) in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component foods are well-characterised in approved sources and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data: yeast-raised glazed donut (NDB 18249) weighted at approximately 60% and cream cheese (NDB 01017) weighted at approximately 40%, reflecting a typical filled donut composition. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entries used for this composite. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entries used for this composite. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entries used for this composite. Vitamin C is 0.0; confirmed zero as neither yeast-raised donut dough nor cream cheese contains meaningful vitamin C per USDA FoodData Central. Halal status is listed as false because Dunkin' Donuts Singapore halal certification status requires verification at the outlet level and cannot be confirmed from available approved sources at time of processing. GI of 76 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database doughnuts entry (Atkinson et al., 2008). Glycemic load per serving = (76 × 32.6) / 100 = 24.8, based on 75g serving size with 43.5g carbohydrate per 100g.

improvement_actions_taken: Expanded food_name from 'Cheesecake Filled' to canonical 'Cheesecake Filled Donut' for precision.
Added all mandatory multilingual local_names for Singapore context: Chinese simplified, Chinese traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi.
Constructed composite nutrient estimate from USDA SR Legacy donut and cream cheese entries with documented weighting.
Assigned GI 76 from University of Sydney GI Database analogous doughnuts entry with full documentation.
Selected FoodOn and FoodEx2 closest_parent codes with honest gap notes; SNOMED CT set to null with gap note.
Documented all -1 sentinel values (vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg) in notes.
Documented vitamin_c_mg = 0.0 as confirmed zero in notes.
Ensured aliases_by_language and flat aliases are perfectly consistent.
Verified gi_category 'high' is correct for GI 76 (>= 70 threshold).
All ontology match_type values use only allowed strings: exact, closest_parent, none.
